blindspots: **Intuit QuickBooks upmarket penetration** — 3M+ customers on AI; multi-year Anthropic partnership. If Intuit's AI genuinely closes the gap on Intacct's mid-market ERP capabilities, pricing power erodes. No direct tracking possible. Proxy: Intacct win rate commentary on earnings calls.
**Finance Intelligence Agent monetization** — Nov 2025 launch, but no disclosure on adoption or pricing. This is the next pricing lever after Copilot. Monitor via: FY2026 interim results commentary.
**CEO succession planning** — Steve Hare is 64. No public succession plan. Mishandled transition could impact execution. Monitor via: annual report governance section.
**SDMO (manufacturing/distribution) TAM capture** — Largest US vertical TAM for Intacct. Early innings. No public metrics on SDMO-specific growth. Monitor via: earnings call product commentary.

cyclical.context: SGE customer base is 6M+ SMBs. Net adds driven by macro conditions. Countercyclical argument: compliance needs persist
cyclical.current: Monitor — UK GDP flat Q1 2026; oil-driven stagflation risk
cyclical.name: SMB formation/failure rates (UK/EU)

cyclical.context: Intacct US revenue in USD; group reports in GBP. Translation effects
cyclical.name: GBP/USD exchange rate

cyclical.context: Tracks management confidence. Pace should be sustainable at 60-70% of FCF
cyclical.current: £1.6B returned FY22-25 (~20% of mcap); £300M FY26 buyback; £498M FCF
cyclical.name: Capital returns

noiseFilter: **Legacy desktop revenue decline** — Expected as cloud transition progresses. Only matters if cloud growth doesn't offset.
**Sage 50 churn headlines** — Sage 50 is mature product. Churn is recycled into Intacct/cloud. Net effect on group revenue is managed.
**Quarterly ARR fluctuations <3%** — Seasonal patterns in SMB buying. Judge on semi-annual trends.
**Intuit competitive announcements** — Intuit talks about moving upmarket every year. Actual Intacct displacement data is what matters, not press releases.

structural.name: Intacct cloud-native growth (YoY)
structural.threshold: <15% = law of large numbers slowing; <10% = structural deceleration; >25% = bull case

structural.name: Organic revenue growth
structural.threshold: <7% for 2+ halves = growth engine weakening; >12% = re-acceleration

structural.name: Cloud-native ARR mix
structural.threshold: <30% = cloud transition stalling; >45% = operating leverage inflection

structural.name: Operating margin trajectory
structural.threshold: <22% = margin regression; >27% = medium-term target in sight (27-30%)

structural.current: +5.5% pricing (FY2025), "particularly helped by AI and Copilot in UK market"
structural.name: AI pricing uplift
structural.threshold: Sustained >4% = AI monetization confirmed; <2% = commoditization risk

structural.name: Net revenue retention (NRR)
structural.threshold: <100% for 2+ periods = customer quality concern; >105% = expansion flywheel working

trendLog.assessment: All indicators on track. Intacct +23%, operating margin 23.9% (6yr trend up), AI pricing +5.5%. RC 0/5 — needs filing + transcript review.

universal.name: Mispricing Assessment
universal.rationale: 40% off ATH; 13x EV/EBITDA; 45-65% bull upside; but well-covered (19 analysts)

universal.name: Repricing Mechanism
universal.rationale: Quarterly earnings mechanically demonstrate thesis; SP-2 pattern

universal.name: Business Quality
universal.rationale: 93% GM, 97% recurring, 101% NRR, 110% FCF conversion, 2% SBC

universal.name: Balance Sheet (GATE)
universal.rationale: Net debt 1.7x EBITDA; FCF £487M; £300M buyback; comfortable
